stem: The equation d = 60t models the distance d, in miles, that Jonas drove from City P to City Q after driving for t hours. Which of the following does 60 represent in the equation?

answerOptions.content: Jonas took 60 trips from City P to City Q.
Jonas drove at an average speed of about 60 miles per hour.
The distance between City P and City Q is 60 miles.
It took Jonas 60 hours to drive from City P to City Q.

rationale: Choice B is correct. In d = 60t, the coefficient 60 represents miles per hour.
